title: classroom50/scores/v1
description: Machine-readable schema for <classroom>/scores.json, the gradebook written by collect_scores.py (cli/gh-teacher/skeleton/dotgithub/scripts/) and read by `gh teacher download`. `submissions` is keyed by assignment slug; each value is that assignment's rows. A row is a result.json (classroom50/result/v1) payload with the `assignment` field DROPPED (it's the bucket key), plus collection-added fields: `late` (boolean, submission datetime vs the assignment `due`) and `override` (boolean teacher flag — `true` rows are preserved verbatim across re-collects). Rules JSON Schema cannot express, enforced in code: (1) within a bucket, rows are keyed by tuple(lowercased usernames) — a key is unique per bucket; (2) the same identity/score cross-field rules as result/v1; (3) the lenient reader also tolerates a legacy flat-array `submissions` and a stray "{}" string, which it normalizes to the keyed map on write — this schema describes only the canonical written form.

submissions.description: Assignment slug -> array of submission rows. `{}` (never null) at scaffold time.

row.description: A result/v1 payload minus `assignment`, plus optional collection-added fields. additionalProperties is true so an autograder's extra top-level diagnostic fields survive ingest verbatim, mirroring entry_from_result.

schema.description: Rows retain the per-result sentinel (not a scores sentinel) — they are stored result payloads.

usernames.description: One entry for an individual submission; the full rostered member list for a group submission (collection rewrites it from the runner's owner-only list).

late.description: Collection-added: true when the submission datetime is after the assignment `due`. Absent when the assignment has no `due`.

override.description: Teacher flag. `true` => the row is preserved verbatim and never refreshed by collection. `false` => an explicit 'keep refreshing' marker that survives replacement.

testResult.description: One graded test. additionalProperties is true: an autograder.py may attach extra diagnostic fields (e.g. output, message) that the runner and collector preserve verbatim.

test-name.description: Human-readable test name (the declarative grader uses the assignment test `name`).

score.description: Points earned on this test. Must be <= this test's max-score (cross-field, enforced in code).
